---
title: "Alejandro Sánchez Barradas"
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}
::: {.profile-left}
![](../images/equipo/alex.png){.profile-photo-large}

::: {.profile-links}
[✉](mailto:alejandro.sanchez@posgrado.ecologia.edu.mx)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-right}
**PhD Student**  
Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (INECOL)

*Interests:* [Biotic interactions]{.profile-tag} [Carnivores]{.profile-tag} [Macroecology]{.profile-tag}

I study how the number of potential prey species and available body size ranges influence the richness of carnivorous mammal species, and whether the predator-prey body size relationship relates to geographic and environmental co-occurrence. I am also highly interested in hypercarnivore trophic ecology, particularly big cats, and its relation to the environmental niche and social behavior of their prey.

### Education

- **PhD** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (ongoing)
- **MSc** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (2018)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-back}
[← Back to Team](../equipo.html)
:::
